parity_goal: Ship the standalone Dot-local inbox intake lane that was removed from jj-journal: safe refresh, quiet day-log filing for distractions, five-field review for remaining items, guarded routing, source archiving, and a short receipt.
behavior_signature_before: Planned as jj-inbox after journal intake removal, but not implemented.
behavior_signature_after: Single default jj-intake mode owns Dot-local intake without mixing into jj-journal or general email cleanup.

expected_output_shape: safe refresh then full local inbox review
obvious distractions quietly filed to the day log
remaining items shown only as Title, Link, Overview, Relevance, and Suggestion
Title
Link
Overview
Relevance
Suggestion
no extra item-review sections or route menu

cases.prompt: $jj-intake and do not bring distractions to me
expected_output_shape: low-priority items logged to current day quiet log
quiet-filed items recorded in receipt
quiet-filed source files moved to handled archive

cases.prompt: $jj-intake handle the first item as a project note
expected_output_shape: full decision record in per-run receipt
destination file path or link recorded
source inbox file moved to handled archive
asks for missing reason or review date
moves source file to deferred archive
does not leave deferred item in active inbox

cases.prompt: $jj-intake draft a reply to this SMS
expected_output_shape: drafted outbound copy only
explicit approval required before send
pending approval shown in final receipt
jj-intake is the intake owner
jj-journal does not promote inbound comms
jj-journal does not read or triage inbox items
